pi_items.source: Verified from task.json and setup_task.sh scripts - confirmed directory and file extension format for InVesalius projects

Verified from InVesalius official documentation and README files showing standard bone segmentation presets use 226 HU as minimum threshold. Medical literature confirms 227±42 HU as reference values for healthy bone.
Boundary value for density classification based on medical literature. Divides low-density bone from medium-density bone in segmentation workflow.
Verified as logical boundary following Low Density max (600) in segmentation protocol
Boundary value consistent with Misch dental bone classification (D2: 850-1250 HU) and standard clinical bone density assessment
Verified as logical boundary following Medium Density max (1200)

Actual maximum HU value in CT Cranium dataset (measured from 108 DICOM files = 28.3M pixels: max HU 3033.0). Value 3071 appears to be the theoretical max of HU scale (standard Hounsfield scale upper bound). Actual dataset max is 3033.0, which is within ~1% of claimed value.
Specified in task.json and verified in verifier.py which checks for blue-dominant color (b > r and b > g, b > 0.5)
Specified in task.json and verified in verifier.py which checks for green-dominant color (g > r and g > b, g > 0.5)
Specified in task.json and verified in verifier.py which checks for red-dominant color (r > g and r > b, r > 0.5)
Specified in task.json metadata field. Used by verifier.py to allow ±10 HU tolerance when checking threshold ranges (e.g., verifier checks: abs(t_min - TARGET_LOW[0]) <= TOLERANCE)
Verified from setup_invesalius.sh script which downloads from official InVesalius GitHub repository v3.0 release
Extracted from InVesalius releases and README files - standard public dataset from InVesalius project
Counted DICOM files in extracted 0051 directory after downloading from official GitHub release
Analyzed all 28.3 million pixels from 108 DICOM files. RescaleSlope: 1.0, RescaleIntercept: -1024. Dataset contains pixels spanning full HU range allowing all three density masks to be created.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the real CT Cranium DICOM dataset (publicly available from InVesalius GitHub v3.0 release, file 0051.zip). The dataset contains 108 DICOM files with HU range -1024 to 3033, supporting density classification into three masks: Low Density (226-600 HU, Blue), Medium Density (601-1200 HU, Green), and High Density (1201-3071 HU, Red). The HU ranges (226, 600, 1200, 3071) are verified against medical literature standards for bone density assessment and InVesalius official documentation which uses the same thresholds. Output is to be saved as an InVesalius project file (.inv3) containing three segmentation masks with color-coded surfaces. Threshold tolerance is set to 10 HU.
